element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to maintain speed and follow criminal vehicle while managing time limit
Player must learn correct route choices to avoid time penalties; incorrect turns lengthen pursuit
Vehicle must be rammed multiple times to force criminal to stop; requires positioning and timing
Time limit extends when criminal vehicle is reached; player must manage ramming phases within extended time windows
Criminals drive increasingly capable vehicles (Lotus Esprit → Lamborghini → Porsche → Ferrari → Porsche 928)
Excessive crashing or slow driving allows criminal to escape; player must balance aggressive pursuit with control

typical_relief_triggers: Time limit extension upon vehicle contact
typical_tension_triggers: Initial time limit countdown (60-70 seconds to reach criminal)
Wrong road choice at splits (extends pursuit time)
Each ramming attempt requiring multiple hits
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Persistent tension with cyclical relief points; initial pursuit phase (60-70s tension) followed by ramming phase (variable duration) with time extension. Tension releases temporarily upon reaching vehicle, then re-escalates during ramming sequence.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Level 1 introduces pursuit and ramming with Lotus Esprit (slowest criminal vehicle); generous time windows allow player learning
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Levels 2-3 escalate with Lamborghini and Porsche 911; road split complexity increases; ramming sequences require more hits
difficulty_curve.late_game: Levels 4-5 feature Ferrari 288 GTO and Eastern Bloc Spy's Porsche 928 (matching player vehicle); minimal time margin for error; complex routing required
notable_spikes: Level 2 transition (Carlos, New York robber, introduces Lamborghini Countach speed)
Level 5 climax (Eastern Bloc Spy drives identical Porsche 928 to player; maximum difficulty)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level follows consistent pattern: timed pursuit phase (60-70s) to reach criminal, then ramming phase with extended time window to force stop and arrest
Each successive level features faster, more capable criminal vehicle, creating natural difficulty progression without level design changes
Road splits require correct navigation choice; wrong turns extend pursuit time and increase criminal escape risk
